meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THIS PLAN UP TO DATE AFTER EVERY SUB-STEP. LOG EVERY DEVIATION, DISCOVERY, AND REPLAN INSIDE THIS FILE BEFORE PROCEEDING. IF REALITY CHANGES, RE-READ THE RELEVANT CODE, THEN REWRITE THE AFFECTED TASKS IMMEDIATELY.
meta.completionPolicy: NO ESTIMATES. NO PRIORITIES. EVERY TASK MUST INCLUDE ITS OWN REPRODUCTION, VALIDATION, STRICT-REVIEW-GATE PASSES, AND A SINGLE SELF-CONTAINED COMMIT BEFORE THE NEXT TASK BEGINS. WHEN THIS LIST IS EXHAUSTED, EXPAND IT AGAIN FROM EVIDENCE, NOT WISHFUL THINKING.

globalAlerts.warning: THE STARTUP RACE IS THE PRIMARY BUG. Fix: Submit initialization BEFORE loop.Run(ctx) when WithAutoExit(true) is enabled.
ASYNC DRAIN IS THE GOAL. All native async Go work MUST use loop.Promisify to be tracked by WithAutoExit. Bare goroutines + loop.Submit() will race with termination and fail.
SOLE EXIT TRIGGER: WithAutoExit(true) must become the primary way the program determines it is 'done' in interactive and scripting modes.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md on every session start. Resume from the first non-Done task.
mandatoryDirectives.toolingConstraints: Use gmake on macOS. Primary signals: make test-short and make lint.

statusSection.currentState: Implementation of WithAutoExit-driven architecture complete. All native modules refactored with Promisify. Core runtime, terminal, and commands updated to wait for natural loop exit. Short tests passing.
statusSection.goalState: one-shot-man uses WithAutoExit(true) as its sole normal-path exit trigger. All async work (shell, TUI, native modules) participates in loop liveness. The startup race is eliminated. Scripts and interactive sessions naturally exit when all work completes.

replanLog.trigger: User directive for CONCRETE solution implementing WithAutoExit architecture.
replanLog.affectedTasks: Tasks 7-8 superseded by new implementation plan.
replanLog.summary: Full re-architecture plan established to move from persistent-runtime model back to auto-exiting model, but with comprehensive lifecycle tracking via Promisify.

sequentialTasks.task: Complete the adversarial autopsy report documents
sequentialTasks.description:
Finalize the scratch/eventloop-autoexit-autopsy-20260430 directory with the missing documents. 
- Create `04_gap_analysis.md`: Detailed breakdown of each subsystem from the inventory and why it's a blocker today.
- Create `06_kill_conditions.md`: Falsifiable list of what MUST be true for auto-exit to be safe (Promisify everywhere, shell tracking, etc.).
- Create `08_conclusions_matrix.md`: The final True/Uncertain/False verdict on the feasibility and required changes.
- Create `README.md`: Scope, reading order, and summary of findings.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All numbered markdown documents (01-09) and README.md exist in the autopsy directory. They are evidence-backed and consistent with the code analyzed in the reconnaissance phase.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix native async modules: fetch and exec Promisify integration
sequentialTasks.description:
Refactor osm:fetch and osm:exec to use loop.Promisify() for all background goroutines. 
- [internal/builtin/fetch/fetch.go]: Wrap the `go func()` in `jsFetch` with `adapter.Loop().Promisify(...)`.
- [internal/builtin/fetch/readable_stream.go]: Wrap the pump goroutine in `Promisify` if it's not already covered.
- [internal/builtin/fetch/sse.go]: Wrap the parser goroutine in `Promisify`.
- [internal/builtin/exec/exec.go]: Wrap `child.Wait()` and `wrapReadableStream` pump goroutines in `Promisify`.
- Verify: `go test -short ./internal/builtin/fetch/... ./internal/builtin/exec/...` passes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Grep no longer finds bare `go func` blocks that call `loop.Submit` in these files. All background work is correctly tracked by the loop's `promisifyCount`.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix native async modules: MCP and MCP Callback Promisify integration
sequentialTasks.description:
Refactor osm:mcp and osm:mcpcallback to use loop.Promisify().
- [internal/builtin/mcpmod/mcp.go]: Wrap `server.Run` goroutine in `jsRun` with `s.loop.Promisify(...)`.
- [internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/mcpcallback.go]: Wrap the listener, accept, and per-connection goroutines with `cb.loop.Promisify(...)` where they call back into the loop.
- Verify: `go test -short ./internal/builtin/mcpmod/... ./internal/builtin/mcpcallbackmod/...` passes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: MCP server and callback lifecycles are represented in the loop's liveness count. `WithAutoExit` will wait for MCP servers to shut down naturally.

sequentialTasks.task: Ensure BubbleTea always participates in loop liveness
sequentialTasks.description:
Refactor [internal/builtin/bubbletea/bubbletea.go] to make Promisify mandatory for `tea.run()`.
- In `tea.run()` (line 2070+), remove the `if manager.promisify != nil` check and ensure the `manager.promisify(...)` path is always taken.
- If `manager.promisify` is nil, it should be considered a configuration error (Engine should always provide it).
- Verify: `go test -short ./internal/builtin/bubbletea/...` passes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All BubbleTea programs started via `tea.run()` increment the loop's `promisifyCount` until they exit.

sequentialTasks.task: Refactor Terminal and TUI Manager for shell liveness tracking
sequentialTasks.description:
The shell/prompt lifecycle must keep the event loop alive.
- [internal/scripting/tui_manager.go]: Update `runAdvancedPrompt` to use `tm.engine.Promisify(...)` around the `p.RunNoExit()` call.
- [internal/scripting/terminal.go]: Ensure `Terminal.Run()` doesn't exit until the loop finishes if it's the primary owner.
- This ensures that while the user is in the interactive shell, `Loop.Alive()` is true.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: An interactive session does not auto-exit as soon as it starts. Typing `exit` (which makes `p.RunNoExit()` return and thus the `Promisify` block finish) allows the loop to exit if no other work is pending.

sequentialTasks.task: Switch to WithAutoExit(true) and fix startup race in Runtime
sequentialTasks.description:
The core architectural change.
- [internal/scripting/runtime.go]:
  - In `NewRuntimeRegistry`, add `goeventloop.WithAutoExit(true)` to `goeventloop.New(...)`.
  - Move the `rt.loop.Submit(...)` for adapter initialization to happen BEFORE the `go rt.loop.Run(rt.ctx)` goroutine is started. This ensures work is visible to the loop on its first check.
  - Remove the now-obsolete `ResolveLiveness()` and other persistent-loop workarounds (if any remain).
- Verify: `go test -short ./internal/scripting/...` passes without 'loop has been terminated' errors.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Runtime uses WithAutoExit(true). Startup race is gone. `make test-short` passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Integrate auto-exit into Engine and Command execution
sequentialTasks.description:
Make the loop's natural exit the signal for program termination.
- [internal/scripting/engine_core.go]: Add `Engine.Wait()` which blocks until the loop naturally returns from `Run()`.
- [internal/command/scripting_command.go] (and others): Update `Execute` to call `engine.Wait()` after `ExecuteScript` (for non-interactive) or after `Terminal.Run()` (for interactive).
- Ensure `Engine.Close()` is still called for cleanup but doesn't preemptively kill the loop during normal auto-exit flows.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A script that calls `setTimeout(() => console.log('done'), 100)` and then finishes its body naturally waits for the 'done' log and then the process exits. No manual `waitForAsyncWork` polling is needed.

sequentialTasks.task: Final Verification: Rule of Two and cross-platform checks
sequentialTasks.description:
Run comprehensive integration tests.
- `make test` (long suite).
- `make lint`.
- Manual verification of: one-shot scripts with async, interactive shell, BubbleTea programs, and MCP bridges.
- Verify on macOS, and ideally trigger CI for others.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Two contiguous issue-free #runSubagent reviews confirm the new architecture is robust and correctly handles all identified lifecycle edge cases.

continuousVerification.task: Run make test-short and make lint after every significant change
